nameMeanings.name: Nash
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: topographic name for someone who lived by an ash tree, a variant of Ash by misdivision of Middle English atten ash ‘at the ash’, or a habitational name from any of the many places in England and Wales named Nash, from this phrase, as for example Nash in Buckinghamshire, Herefordshire, or Shropshire. The name was established from an early date in Wales and Ireland.
Jewish: possibly an Americanized form of one or more similar (like-sounding) Jewish surnames.

History: The surname Nash was taken to Ireland from England or Wales by a family who established themselves in County Kerry in the 13th century, during the second wave of Anglo-Norman settlement. — Abner Nash (c. 1740–86), governor of NC, was of Welsh origin, his parents having emigrated to VA from Wales in 1730. His brother Francis (c. 1742–77) was a general in the Continental army; the city of Nashville, TN, was named in his honor.

variants.name: Naish
meanings: English (mainly Wiltshire, Somerset, and Gloucestershire): variant of   Nash   1.

variants.name: Ash
meanings: English: from Middle English asche ‘ash tree’ (Old English æsc), hence a topographic name for someone living by an ash tree or a habitational name from any of the many places in southern and central England named with this word (Derbyshire, Dorset, Hampshire, Herefordshire, Kent, Surrey, Shropshire, Somerset, and elsewhere).
Americanized form of German   Asch   and   Esch  , the latter ultimately also of Swiss German origin (see   Oesch  ).
Americanized form (translation into English) of French   Dufresne  , with the same meaning as 1 above.

nameMeanings.name: Margaret
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

variants.name: Margareta
meanings: From the Latin form of Greek Margarītēs. See Margaret .

variants.name: Margaretta
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

historicEvents.title: State Lottery
historicEvents.description: A State Lottery was recorded in 1569. The tickets were sold at St. Paul's Cathedral in London.

historicEvents.title: The Royal Exchange is Organized
historicEvents.description: The Royal Exchange was organized in January 1571 by Sir Thomas Gresham. Gresham is known as the father of English banking.

historicEvents.title: The First English Colony is Established in America
historicEvents.description: England established its first colony in North America in 1585 as a way to generate additional wealth. The colony was named Virginia, after the virgin Queen Elizabeth I.

historicEvents.title: Elizabeth I Signs Death Warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots
historicEvents.description: Elizabeth I signed the death warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots, on February 1, 1587, as she was found guilty of plotting to assasinate Elizabeth. Mary, Queen of Scots, was beheaded at Fotheringhay Castle in England on February 8, 1587.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Elizabeth I.org - Mary Queen of Scots

en.lifeSummary: Margaret Nash was born in 1552, in Woolpit, Suffolk, England. She married Thomas Wither Wyther (4th Earl of Stonham) in 1567, in Oldbury upon Severn, Gloucestershire, England, United Kingdom. She died on 5 November 1615, in her hometown, at the age of 63, and was buried in Woolpit, Suffolk, England.

Elle a épousé Thomas Wither Wyther (4th Earl of Stonham) en 1567, à Oldbury upon Severn, Gloucestershire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Elle est décédée le 5 novembre 1615, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 63 ans, et a été enterrée à Woolpit, Suffolk, Angleterre.

ko.Oldbury Upon Severn,Gloucester,England: 영국 잉글랜드 그루스스터실르 드버이 업 세버
ko.lifeSummary: Margaret Nash(은)는 1552년 영국 수폴크 보피에서 태어났다. 그녀는 1567년 영국 잉글랜드 그루스스터실르 드버이 업 세버에서 Thomas Wither Wyther (4th Earl of Stonham)(와)과 결혼했다. 그녀는 1615년 11월 5일 그녀의 고향에서 63세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 수폴크 보피에 묻혔다.

ru.lifeSummary: Margaret Nash родилась в 1552 г. в местности вулпит, суффолк, Англия. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Thomas Wither Wyther (4th Earl of Stonham) в 1567 г. в местности олдбури упон сивирн, глоусестиршир, Англия, Великобритания. Она умерла 5 ноября 1615 г. в её родном городе в возрасте 63 лет и была похоронена в местности вулпит, суффолк, Англия.
